mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor demand that triggers corporate earnings revisions downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0% within a single month.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in real GDP growth and a spike in the CPIAUCSL index compresses real disposable income, forcing equity risk premiums higher and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns if the realized 60-day forward return correlation with the composite index exceeds 0.15 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight capacity utilization, a decline in the industrial production index signals binding supply constraints that erode corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism is rejected if SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant decline when INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average during high-capacity-utilization regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts exceeding 10% from the prior month acts as a leading indicator of broader consumer demand contraction, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative in the top quartile of HOUST monthly declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the PAYEMS level rises above 154,000 while the UNRATE simultaneously increases, it indicates a surge in labor force participation that dilutes wage pressure and signals weakening demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 154,000 and UNRATE rises simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in revolving consumer credit balances reflects increasing household leverage that fuels near-term consumption spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism is falsified if 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive during quarters where RSAFS shows a positive 3-month moving average slope.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% while the unemployment rate remains below 4.0%, it signals an overly restrictive policy stance that will eventually force a recessionary earnings cut, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%.
